content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers A,BA, BA,B. You have to choose an integer XXX in the range [minimum(A,BA, BA,B), maximum(A,BA, BA,B)] such that:

‚ĆąB‚ąíX2‚ĆČ+‚ĆąX‚ąíA2‚ĆČ\Big \lceil \frac{B - X}{2} \Big \rceil + \Big \lceil \frac{X - A}{2} \Big \rceil‚Ćą2B‚ąíX‚Äč‚ĆČ+‚Ćą2X‚ąíA‚Äč‚ĆČ is maximum.

What is the maximum sum you can obtain if you choose XXX optimally?

**Note:** ‚Ćąx‚ĆČ\lceil x \rceil‚Ćąx‚ĆČ : Returns the smallest integer that is greater than or equal to xxx (i.e  rounds up to the nearest integer). For example, ‚Ćą1.4‚ĆČ=2\lceil 1.4 \rceil = 2‚Ćą1.4‚ĆČ=2, ‚Ćą5‚ĆČ=5\lceil 5 \rceil = 5‚Ćą5‚ĆČ=5, ‚Ćą‚ąí1.5‚ĆČ=‚ąí1\lceil -1.5 \rceil = -1‚Ćą‚ąí1.5‚ĆČ=‚ąí1, ‚Ćą‚ąí3‚ĆČ=‚ąí3\lceil -3 \rceil = -3‚Ćą‚ąí3‚ĆČ=‚ąí3 , ‚Ćą0‚ĆČ=0\lceil 0 \rceil = 0‚Ćą0‚ĆČ=0.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, two integers A,BA, BA,B.


Output Format
For each testcase, output the maximum sum of ‚ĆąB‚ąíX2‚ĆČ+‚ĆąX‚ąíA2‚ĆČ\lceil \frac{B - X}{2} \rceil + \lceil \frac{X - A}{2} \rceil‚Ćą2B‚ąíX‚Äč‚ĆČ+‚Ćą2X‚ąíA‚Äč‚ĆČ.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, there is only one possible value of XXX which is equal to 222. So the sum is equal to ‚Ćą2‚ąí22‚ĆČ+‚Ćą2‚ąí22‚ĆČ\lceil \frac{2 - 2}{2} \rceil + \lceil \frac{2 - 2}{2} \rceil‚Ćą22‚ąí2‚Äč‚ĆČ+‚Ćą22‚ąí2‚Äč‚ĆČ = ‚Ćą¬†0‚ĆČ+‚Ćą¬†0‚ĆČ\lceil \ 0 \rceil + \lceil \ 0 \rceil‚Ćą¬†0‚ĆČ+‚Ćą¬†0‚ĆČ  = 0+0=00 + 0 = 00+0=0.


In the second test case, we can choose XXX to be 222. So sum is equal to ‚Ćą11‚ąí22‚ĆČ+‚Ćą2‚ąí12‚ĆČ\lceil \frac{11 - 2}{2} \rceil + \lceil \frac{2 - 1}{2} \rceil‚Ćą211‚ąí2‚Äč‚ĆČ+‚Ćą22‚ąí1‚Äč‚ĆČ = ‚Ćą¬†4.5‚ĆČ+‚Ćą¬†0.5‚ĆČ\lceil \ 4.5 \rceil + \lceil \ 0.5 \rceil‚Ćą¬†4.5‚ĆČ+‚Ćą¬†0.5‚ĆČ = 5+1=65 + 1 = 65+1=6. There is no possible way to choose an integer XXX in the range [1,11][1, 11][1,11] such that sum is greater than 666.
